title: Canthium hispidonervosum
intro: Canthium hispidonervosum, also known as the "wild forest tea tree," is a species of plant in the Loganiaceae family, commonly found in tropical regions. The leaves and stems of C. hispidonervosum have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, providing relief from various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of C. hispidonervosum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, C. hispidonervosum is often prepared into infusions, teas, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory infections, fever, and other health conditions.
taxonomy: Canthium hispidonervosum belongs to the family Rubiaceae. This family consists of flowering plants commonly known as coffee or madder family.
